title: A neighbor's dog I helped
summary: Narrate a time you helped a neighbor's dog in some way.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a time you helped a neighbor's dog. What happened? How did you help? How did you feel about it? Use past simple and past continuous. Use when, while, because, and although to connect your ideas.
hintStarters: One afternoon I heard a dog barking outside and…
Although I was a little nervous at first, I…
sampleText: One evening last October, I was walking home through my neighbourhood in Lisbon when I heard a dog whimpering near a gate. I stopped and looked. A medium-sized brown dog was sitting in the street. It was trembling and its leash was caught on a metal spike on the fence. The dog was alone and could not move. It looked scared but did not bark at me. I crouched down slowly and spoke to it in a soft voice. The dog stopped trembling a little. I gently unhooked the leash from the spike. The dog stood up and wagged its tail. While I was trying to read the phone number on its collar, my neighbour came running around the corner. She was very relieved. She explained that the dog had got loose from the garden while she was inside for just a few minutes. She thanked me many times. The dog jumped up and licked my hand, which made both of us laugh. Although I was only outside for five minutes, I felt good for the rest of the evening. It was not a difficult thing to do, but it mattered to the dog and to my neighbour. I think small acts of help like that are easy to give but not always easy to remember to give.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: heard a dog whimpering near a gate
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nghe thấy tiếng chó rên rỉ gần cổng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: its leash was caught on a metal spike
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: dây dắt của nó bị mắc vào đinh nhọn kim loại

sampleAnnotations.phrase: spoke to it in a soft voice
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nói chuyện với nó bằng giọng nhẹ nhàng
nhẹ nhàng gỡ dây dắt ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: trying to read the phone number on its collar
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cố đọc số điện thoại trên vòng cổ của nó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: it mattered to the dog and to my neighbour
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: điều đó có ý nghĩa với con chó và hàng xóm của tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for interrupted or background actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous để nói về hành động đang diễn ra khi một sự kiện khác xảy ra hoặc để làm nền bối cảnh: 'I was walking home when I heard...', 'While I was trying to read the collar, my neighbour came.' Past simple diễn đạt sự kiện ngắn gọn hơn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous for actions in progress when another event happened: 'I was walking home when I heard...', 'While I was trying to read the collar, my neighbour came.' Past simple expresses the shorter, complete events.

sampleTranslationVi: Một buổi tối tháng Mười năm ngoái, tôi đang đi bộ về nhà qua khu phố ở Lisbon thì nghe thấy tiếng chó rên rỉ gần cổng. Tôi dừng lại và nhìn. Một con chó màu nâu cỡ trung đang ngồi dưới đường. Nó đang run rẩy và dây dắt của nó bị mắc vào đinh nhọn kim loại trên hàng rào. Con chó một mình và không thể di chuyển. Nó trông có vẻ sợ hãi nhưng không sủa vào tôi. Tôi từ từ ngồi xổm xuống và nói chuyện với nó bằng giọng nhẹ nhàng. Con chó ngừng run rẩy một chút. Tôi nhẹ nhàng gỡ dây dắt ra khỏi đinh nhọn. Con chó đứng dậy và vẫy đuôi. Trong khi tôi đang cố đọc số điện thoại trên vòng cổ của nó, hàng xóm tôi chạy vòng qua góc đường. Bà rất nhẹ nhõm. Bà giải thích rằng con chó đã sổng ra khỏi vườn trong khi bà vào trong chỉ vài phút. Bà cảm ơn tôi nhiều lần. Con chó nhảy lên và liếm tay tôi, khiến cả hai chúng tôi cười. Dù tôi chỉ ở ngoài năm phút, tôi cảm thấy tốt suốt phần còn lại của buổi tối. Đó không phải điều khó làm, nhưng nó có ý nghĩa với con chó và hàng xóm của tôi. Tôi nghĩ những hành động giúp đỡ nhỏ như vậy dễ cho đi nhưng không phải lúc nào cũng dễ nhớ để làm.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'when' to connect two past actions?
options: I walked home when I was hearing a dog whimpering.
I am walking home when I heard a dog whimpering.
I was walking home when I was hearing a dog whimpering.
I was walking home when I heard a dog whimpering.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Was walking' is the ongoing background; 'heard' is the interrupting completed event.
mcQuestions.hint: Past continuous + when + past simple for an interrupted action.

options: to make soft, sad sounds, usually from pain or fear
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Whimper' means to make quiet, sad sounds, as an animal does when in pain or scared.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes a soft, sad sound an animal makes?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'while': '___ I was trying to read the collar, my neighbour arrived.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While' + past continuous introduces the background action during which another event happened.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'while' + past continuous for an ongoing action.

options: a soft blanket for a pet
a long cord or strap used to control a dog
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'leash' is a cord attached to a dog's collar to control it during walks.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the word for the cord you hold when walking a dog?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to bend your knees and lower your body'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Crouch down' means to lower your body by bending your knees.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase describes bending your knees to get lower?

options: to move the tail from side to side, showing happiness
to sit still and look up
mcQuestions.explanation: Dogs 'wag their tails' by moving them quickly from side to side when happy.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase describes a happy dog moving its tail?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'although': '___ I was only outside for five minutes, I felt good.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a contrast: a short time outside led to a lasting good feeling.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word shows an unexpected contrast?

options: to escape from a restricted place
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Get loose' means to escape from something that was holding you.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means 'to escape from a confined space'?

options: a feeling of happiness after a worry ends
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Relief' is the pleasant feeling when a worry or difficulty is over.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes the feeling when a problem is finally over?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'The dog ___ up and licked my hand.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'jumped' is correct for a completed action in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple for a completed action.

options: in a fast and rough way
in a soft and careful way
in a loud and sudden way
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Gently' means in a soft, careful, or kind manner.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adverb means 'in a soft and careful way'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think people in cities pay enough attention to animals in their neighbourhood? What do you think people can do to make their streets safer for pets?
